description: Shared mismatch policies for Suka Smart Assistant location presets. Reuse these in garden_beds.json, garden_animal_access.json, animal_facilities.json, preservation_zones.json, seasonal_structures.json, and routes_and_paths.json so you don’t repeat logic. Policies tell the UI what to do when the user picks an out-of-zone plant/animal/location, and how to automatically generate extra cleaning/garden/storehouse tasks to make it safe.

notes: Use policyId, not raw inline policy, in your location presets where possible.
If scraper/import finds a site-specific rule (ex: for a certain seed supplier), we can map it to one of these by code.
All policies support user favorites so a household can say, ‘Yes, I accept the extra work for this plant/animal.’

policies.label: Warn & Suggest Alternates
policies.description: Let the user do it, but tell them it’s not ideal. Show 2–4 better choices that match zone/species/facility, and add a small cleaning/garden follow-up.

extraTasks.why: out-of-zone or risky choice needs extra sanitation

notes: Best for out-of-season plant in a slightly warmer zone.
Best for goats in light garden areas when you *really* wanted poultry.
Best for using a feast/sukkot structure outside its main months when weather is still ok.

policies.label: Block & Suggest Alternates
policies.description: Too risky to run here: wrong climate, wrong facility, or would contaminate harvest zone. Don’t allow proceed, but show safer alternates the user can swap in.

extraTasks.why: we tried to put the wrong animal/plant here; run care to restore bed

notes: Use this for big-hoof animals on soft raised beds.
Use this when user tries to process cattle in small outdoor bay not rated for large animals.
Use this when user tries to store meat in a non-cold preservation zone.

policies.label: Allow with Mitigation
policies.description: Let the user do the risky thing but auto-attach work: cleaning, storehouse buffer, water, shade, pest control. This is your main ‘graceful degradation’ policy.

extraTasks.why: out-of-zone animals/crops usually need more water

notes: Use this for heat-sensitive animals in hot zone (add shade/mister).
Use this for cool-weather crops in 8b (add shade cloth + extra water).
Use this for running poultry tractors closer to garden than recommended (add cleaning).

policies.label: Sabbath Guarded – Block
policies.description: Respect sabbathGuard and do not run heavy/risky sessions during Sabbath. Offer to reschedule to prep day, and still keep reverse-gen chain intact.
notes: Use this when a user tries to run butchery or hot canning on Sabbath.
Use this when the user tries to do heavy outdoor cleaning during Sabbath.

policies.label: Quiet Hours – Defer
policies.description: User can do it, but noises/odors/vibration make it a quiet-hours violation. Offer to run at next available slot (automation/runtime decides).
notes: Use this for dehydrator on porch at midnight.
Use this for loud poultry processing when people are sleeping.

policies.label: Hub Policy – Strict Compliance
policies.description: When the Suka Village Family Fund Hub says ‘no’, households must use hub-provided alternates. All out-of-band attempts are logged.
notes: Use this for high-value shared greenhouses, walk-in freezers, co-op animal facilities.
Use this for seasonal structures that the hub centrally manages (feast/tent areas).

favoritesTemplate.label: My ‘I’ll do the extra work’ Policy
